content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); vertex 111 is the root. Initially, for each valid iii, the iii-th vertex has a weight wiw_iwi‚Äč. You should perform a sequence of QQQ operations of the following two types:


Add xxx to the weight of vertex vvv.
Add xxx to the weight of each vertex in the subtree of vertex vvv (including vvv).
Let's define a function f(u)=‚ąĎi=1Nwi‚čÖd(i,u)f(u) = \sum_{i=1}^N w_i \cdot d(i, u)f(u)=‚ąĎi=1N‚Äčwi‚Äč‚čÖd(i,u), where d(i,u)d(i, u)d(i,u) denotes the distance between vertices iii and uuu. After each operation, find an integer uuu such that f(u)f(u)f(u) is minimum amoung f(1),f(2),‚Ä¶,f(N)f(1), f(2), \ldots, f(N)f(1),f(2),‚Ä¶,f(N), if there are multiple values of uuu find smallest one.
constraints: the weights of all vertices remain positive after each operation
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers w1,w2,‚Ä¶,wNw_1, w_2, \ldots, w_Nw1‚Äč,w2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,wN‚Äč.
N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains three space-separated integers ttt, vvv and xxx describing one operation, where ttt denotes the type of the operation.
content.outputFormat: Print QQQ lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain one integer uuu such that f(u)f(u)f(u) is minimum among f(1),f(2),‚Ä¶,f(N)f(1), f(2), \ldots, f(N)f(1),f(2),‚Ä¶,f(N) after the iii-th operation, if there are multiple values of uuu find smallest one.
